explanation:
Building requires technical calculations: concrete, steel, bricks, plaster, waterproofing. Standard agencies like IRAM and CAC help standardize construction processes.

## How It's Calculated

Optimal stair dimensions follow Blondel's Rule (62-64 cm) · Free and instant.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting when values fall outside safe building limits.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a specialized professional. Values are current to 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is Blondel's Rule for stairs?
faq.a: Tread + 2(Riser) = 62-64 cm. This formula ensures stairs are both comfortable and safe.

faq.q: What is the ideal stair riser height?
faq.a: 17-18 cm is ideal. Commercial buildings allow up to 20 cm maximum, minimum 16 cm for comfort.

faq.q: What should stair tread depth be?
faq.a: 28-30 cm is ideal. Shallower treads feel cramped, deeper ones are tiring. Blondel's Rule balances both.

faq.q: What's the maximum safe staircase angle?
faq.a: 35-37° is the maximum. Steeper stairs are unsafe and uncomfortable to climb.

faq.q: How do I design spiral stairs?
faq.a: Spiral stairs require a minimum 1.5 m diameter per building code. Apply Blondel's Rule to the inner radius.
